context: Tests one-question-at-a-time formatting and gap identification for a greenfield feature with no existing specs.

checklist.name: Output file created
checklist.description: A file named webhook-interview-prep.md is produced

checklist.name: Questions are individual items
checklist.description: Interview questions are listed as separate numbered items — each question asks about exactly ONE topic, not bundled (e.g. NOT 'What about retries, timeouts, and ordering?')

checklist.name: Questions are self-contained
checklist.description: Each question makes sense on its own without needing to read previous questions — no 'follow-up to above' or 'related to Q3' references

checklist.name: Questions ordered by criticality
checklist.description: Core behavior questions (what events, delivery guarantees, URL configuration) come before edge case questions (rate limiting, payload size, etc.)

checklist.name: Questions are specific and bounded
checklist.description: Questions are concrete (e.g. 'Should failed deliveries be retried, and if so, how many times?' not 'What about error handling?')

checklist.name: Delivery semantics addressed
checklist.description: Gap list or questions address delivery guarantees — at-least-once vs. at-most-once vs. exactly-once

checklist.name: Retry/failure handling addressed
checklist.description: Gap list or questions address what happens when a webhook delivery fails (retries, backoff, disabling endpoints)

checklist.name: Security addressed
checklist.description: Gap list or questions address webhook security (signing/verification, HTTPS requirements, secret management)

checklist.name: No spec created
checklist.description: No .spec.md file is created — this is pre-interview preparation only

checklist.name: No implementation proposed
checklist.description: Document does NOT propose technical solutions, architecture choices, or implementation details (e.g. does NOT say 'use a message queue' or 'store in PostgreSQL')
